votes.rationale: Recomputed totals matched the pre-calculated weighted totals exactly on the tested differentiated-score input (SLOT_LIBRARIAN_01: 70.5, SLOT_LIBRARIAN_02: 51.0). Ranking and winner determination behaved correctly. No code changes were needed. The tested case supports confidence in the shared scoring path on the 2-candidate Phase 1 shape.

The proof target was correctly bounded to one explicit differentiated-score distribution with pre-calculated expectations. The motion is honest that only one panel was runtime-tested directly, that broader panel equivalence is a supported inference from shared code, and that edge cases such as tied totals and boundary scores remain unproven. These are appropriate targets for future motions.

The differentiated-score behavior proof extends the governed confirmation from zero-score path validation to realistic weighted scoring. Evidence is calibrated: one panel runtime-tested, pre-calculated expectations matched exactly, shared scoring core supports broader inference. The proof chain from motion-0046 through motion-0051 is internally consistent and each layer is cleanly bounded.
